perturbation.original_text: I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with all requirements of the PCII program set out in the Critical Infrastructure Information Act of 2002 (CII Act) (Title II, Subtitle B, of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, Public Law 107-296, 196 Stat. 2135, 6 USC 101 et seq.), as amended, the implementing regulations thereto (6 CFR Part 29), as amended, and the applicable PCII Procedures Manual, as amended, and with any such requirements that may be officially communicated to me by the PCII Program Manager or the PCII Program Manager’s designee.
perturbation.changed_text: I attest that I am generally familiar with the PCII program and will endeavor to comply with the requirements of the Critical Infrastructure Information Act of 2002 (CII Act) (Title II, Subtitle B, of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, Public Law 107-296, 196 Stat. 2135, 6 USC 101 et seq.), as amended, the implementing regulations thereto (6 CFR Part 29), as amended, and the applicable PCII Procedures Manual, as amended, and with any such requirements that may be communicated to me.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "I will comply with all requirements" to "I will endeavor to comply" introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation to strictly adhere to the PCII program's requirements. The original text mandates strict adherence while the change suggests a less stringent, best-effort approach.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Critical Infrastructure Information Act of 2002 and 6 CFR Part 29
perturbation.law_explanation: 6 CFR § 29.5 outlines the responsibilities of PCII holders, which includes safeguarding and using the information strictly in accordance with the Act. The modified text weakens this obligation by only requiring an 'endeavor' to comply, which contradicts the mandated strict compliance.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates strict adherence to the PCII program requirements. The changed text weakens this by stating the party will 'endeavor to comply,' suggesting a best-effort approach rather than strict compliance. The Law Explanation also supports this and notes that 6 CFR § 29.5 outlines the responsibilities of PCII holders, which includes safeguarding and using the information strictly in accordance with the Act. While only snippet 1 is available, it refers to 6 CFR 29.5, and therefore, indirectly supports the claim that 'endeavor' weakens a strict compliance mandate.

perturbation.original_text: I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding of SSI information as cited in this Agreement and in accordance with 49 CFR Part 1520, “Protection of Sensitive Security Information,” “Policies and Procedures for Safeguarding and Control of SSI,” as amended, and any supplementary guidance issued by an authorized official of the Department of Homeland Security.
perturbation.changed_text: I attest that I am reasonably familiar with, and I will attempt to comply with the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding of SSI information as generally described in this Agreement and with reference to 49 CFR Part 1520, “Protection of Sensitive Security Information,” “Policies and Procedures for Safeguarding and Control of SSI,” as amended, and any supplementary guidance issued by an authorized official of the Department of Homeland Security.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "I am familiar with" to "I am reasonably familiar with" and "I will comply with" to "I will attempt to comply with" introduces ambiguity regarding the level of knowledge and commitment required for handling Sensitive Security Information (SSI). This weakens the obligation to fully adhere to the standards set forth in 49 CFR Part 1520.
perturbation.law_explanation: 49 CFR § 1520.7 mandates specific requirements for handling SSI, including strict control over access and dissemination. The modified text introduces ambiguity with 'attempt to comply,' which could be interpreted as not requiring full compliance, thus contradicting the regulation's strict mandates.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates full compliance with SSI standards, referencing 49 CFR Part 1520. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating the individual will 'attempt to comply' and is only 'reasonably familiar' with the standards. The Law Explanation correctly states that 49 CFR § 1520.7 mandates specific requirements for handling SSI, including strict control over access and dissemination. The changed text introduces ambiguity by using 'attempt to comply,' which can be interpreted as not requiring full compliance. This contradicts the regulation's strict mandates. Although only one snippet was provided, it refers directly to the code of federal regulations. The original text created a hard legal obligation, whereas the changed text created a soft legal obligation, which would likely fail legal scrutiny.

perturbation.original_text: I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding of the information to which I am granted access as cited in this Agreement and in accordance with the guidance provided to me relative to the specific category of information.
perturbation.changed_text: I attest that I am aware of, and I will consider the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding of the information to which I am granted access as generally outlined in this Agreement and with consideration of the guidance provided to me relative to the specific category of information.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "I am familiar with" with "I am aware of" and "I will comply with" with "I will consider" weakens the user's obligation to fully adhere to the outlined standards. 'Consider' implies discretion rather than strict adherence to the standards for handling sensitive but unclassified information.
perturbation.law_explanation: 5 U.S. Code § 552a, the Privacy Act, establishes standards for the handling of personal information. The modification suggests the user may only 'consider' the standards, opening the door to potential misuse or unauthorized disclosure, which is a violation of the Act’s core intent to safeguard personal information.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 5 / PART I / CHAPTER 5 / SUBCHAPTER II / § 552a Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 5 USC 552a : Records maintained on individuals Text contains those laws in effect on July 12, 2025 From Title 5-GOVERNMENT ORGANIZATION AND EMPLOYEES PART I-THE AGENCIES GENERALLY CHAPTER 5-ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURE SUBCHAPTER II-ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURE Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous References In Text Codification Amendments Change of Name Effective Date Short Title Termination Date Regulations Executive Documents §552a. Records maintained on individuals (a) Definitions .-For purposes of this section- (1) the term "agency" means agency as defined in section 552(e) 1 of this title; (2) the term "individual" means a citizen of the United States or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence; (3) the term "maintain" includes maintain, collect, use, or disseminate; (4) the term "record" means any item, collection, or grouping of information about an individual that is maintained by an agency, including, but not limited to, his education, financial transactions, medical history, and criminal or employment history and that contains his name, or the identifying number, symbol, or other identifying particular assigned to the individual, such as a finger or voice print or a photograph; (5) the term "system of records" means a group of any records under the control of any agency from which information is retrieved by the name of the individual or by some identifying number, symbol, or other identifying particular assigned to the individual; (6) the term "statistical record" means a record in a system of records maintained for statistical research or reporting purposes only and not used in whole or in part in making any determination about an identifiable individual, except as provided by section 8 of title 13 ; (7) the term "routine use" means, with respect to the disclosure of a record, the use of such record for a purpose which is compatible with the purpose for which it was collected; (8) the term "matching program"- (A) means any computerized comparison of- (i) two or more automated systems of records or a system of records with non-Federal records for the purpose of- (I) establishing or verifying the eligibility of, or continuing compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements by, applicants for, recipients or beneficiaries of, participants in, or providers of services with respect to, cash or in-kind assistance or payments under Federal benefit programs, or (II) recouping payments or delinquent debts under such Federal benefit programs, or (ii) two or more automated Federal personnel or payroll systems of records
perturbation.justification: The change from 'I will comply with' to 'I will consider' significantly weakens the obligation to adhere to information handling standards. 5 U.S. Code § 552a establishes standards for handling personal information, and stating one will 'consider' rather than 'comply' directly contradicts the intent to safeguard personal information. While snippet 2 is missing, snippet 1 defines 'maintain' to include 'collect, use, or disseminate', highlighting the active role expected in protecting records, an expectation undermined by the weaker 'consider' obligation.
